intro: The biggest real split between Plain and HubSpot is lock-in posture: Plain carries medium lock-in (seat-based, data exportable via api), while HubSpot carries high lock-in (deep crm integration, data exportable). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Plain is built for apps and is not a fit for products without customer support. HubSpot is built for apps and is not a fit for products without customer support. On the scoring side, Plain rates 4/5 for developer experience and 4/5 for transparency; HubSpot rates 5/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Plain you accept that products without customer support, while HubSpot comes with the reality that products without customer support. Neither side is free. Plain's headline strength in the data is that in-app customer support chat. HubSpot's headline strength is that in-app customer support chat. Read each side's best-for and not-for fields carefully before you pick; the shape of your team and workflow matters more than any single score.
quick_take: Plain is for apps; HubSpot is for apps; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Plain if you are apps and can accept medium lock-in (seat-based, data exportable via api).
verdict_b: Choose HubSpot if you are apps and need high lock-in (deep crm integration, data exportable).

faq.a: Both list a seat pricing model in our data. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Plain to HubSpot?
faq.a: Plain has medium lock-in (seat-based, data exportable via api); HubSpot has high lock-in (deep crm integration, data exportable). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Plain; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
In our data, HubSpot scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Plain. That said, DX is subjective; the Plain score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is HubSpot a good alternative to Plain?
faq.a: HubSpot is built for apps and explicitly not for products without customer support, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Plain for apps, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
